description: Richmond Civic Hackathon — Thriving and Inclusive Communities Pillar

files.title: Thriving and Inclusive Communities — Decision Funnel
files.summary: Root navigation document covering decision phases, repo map, guardrails, and Hackbot resources for the pillar.

files.title: Quick Start
files.summary: 15–30 minute orientation guide for new hackathon teams joining this pillar.

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for Hackbot AI assistant: boot sequence, skill system, research corpus navigation, guardrails, and decision logic.

files.title: Claude Code Configuration
files.summary: Role definition and operating rules for Claude acting as the Thriving and Inclusive Communities pillar research guide.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Canonical orientation for the research corpus: what it contains, source-of-truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and AI agent instructions.

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Step-by-step guide for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports and metadata files to keep the corpus consistent and navigable.

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: Boot prompt and context initialization instructions for the Hackbot AI assistant.

files.title: Evidence Log
files.summary: Tracks verified factual claims and documented information gaps for the Thriving and Inclusive Communities corpus. The Missing section is authoritative for what data is NOT available.

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the Thriving and Inclusive Communities pillar, covering scope, community focus, and connection to Richmond's MAP framework.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: Documents how the pillar's problem statements align with Richmond's MAP strategic framework and departmental priorities.

files.title: Blue Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broader, aspirational problem statements for the pillar before scoring and scoping. Useful for ideation but lower data-readiness than targeted statements.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements
files.summary: Scored problem statements for this pillar: Immigrant Service Discovery (24/32, Strong) and Cross-Agency Navigation (22/32, Needs work). Primary reference for choosing a hackathon problem.

files.title: Data Source Index
files.summary: Index of all data sources identified for the Thriving and Inclusive Communities pillar, with access mode, key fields, and verification status.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: Machine-readable CSV inventory of data sources with access type, URL, key fields, and verification status. Mirror of 00_index.md in tabular format.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Synthesized user journey maps for primary personas (immigrant resident, case manager, community organization) seeking services in Richmond.

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Synthesized recommendations for prototype directions, derived from research and opportunity analysis.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Comparative scan of existing tools and benchmarks relevant to immigrant service access and community navigation.

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon continuation pathways for pillar projects, including potential partners, artifacts worth continuing, and next steps.

files.title: Community Trust Framework
files.summary: Authoritative privacy and trust design requirements for tools serving Richmond's immigrant and refugee communities. Covers PII avoidance, consent design, and safe data practices. Must be consulted before any data collection design.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Describes the viable MVP shapes for this pillar, with tradeoffs, data requirements, and recommended starting points for each direction.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture patterns recommended for hackathon prototypes in this pillar, balancing speed, safety, and demo quality.

files.title: Data Prep Guide
files.summary: Guide for preparing usable datasets for the hackathon prototype, including the manual Help1RVA extraction approach and org directory assembly.

files.title: Risk Review Checklist
files.summary: Checklist for reviewing a prototype against the pillar's risk criteria, including privacy, language quality, false safety, and eligibility overclaiming.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Advice for preparing and delivering a compelling hackathon demo for this pillar, including trust signals, user story framing, and honest scope acknowledgment.

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Reconstructs repository state by scanning key project artifacts and determining the current stage of team work.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Helps teams analyze problem statements, rank opportunities, and identify realistic buildable directions for the hackathon.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Executes research prompts, extracts sources, and summarizes evidence for hackathon teams.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Scans data directory, identifies usable schemas and datasets, and highlights gaps for prototype development.

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Translates research findings into buildable solution opportunities, matching problems to viable MVP shapes.

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Turns a chosen opportunity into a concrete MVP with user flow, architecture, and scope boundaries.

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Reviews prototypes against civic-tech risk criteria including hallucinated services, PII exposure, eligibility claims, and language quality failures.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Helps teams prepare effective demo scripts, narrative structures, and judge-facing explanations.

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Helps users navigate repository knowledge by answering location questions about datasets, problem statements, and templates.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Identifies post-hackathon continuation opportunities including potential partners, artifacts worth continuing, and recommended next steps.

files.title: Skill: research_search
files.summary: Searches the research corpus for relevant content to answer team questions, using index.json and section structure.

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Maps a user question to the correct section and specific research reports before answering, preventing context gaps and hallucination.

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Synthesizes findings across multiple research reports with source traceability, tension identification, gap documentation, and per-claim confidence levels.

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Answers questions using only content present in the corpus, with a 4-level claim classification system and anti-fabrication checks.

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Safely adds or updates research reports and keeps all metadata, indexes, and navigation files synchronized.

files.title: Template: Decision Memo
files.summary: Template for documenting a team's problem selection decision, including chosen problem, user, rationale, and privacy approach.

files.title: Template: Project One-Pager
files.summary: Template for the project one-pager used during scope lock, covering must-haves, data plan, AI role, privacy decisions, and demo path.

files.title: Template: Team Profile
files.summary: Template for creating a team profile file that configures Hackbot with team member roles, skills, and communication preferences.

files.title: Template: Working Direction Note
files.summary: Template for capturing a team's working direction during rapid orientation, covering candidate problem, likely user, weekend buildability, and privacy risk.

files.title: Research Corpus Index
files.summary: Machine-readable index of all 51 research reports in research/. Each entry has id, section, title, summary, and key_terms. Use this for keyword search before reading source files.
